### **5.3 Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)** {#5.3-work-breakdown-structure-(wbs)}

![This diagram illustrates a user account management system, featuring various user roles and features, such as user registration, account management, stock monitoring, and data analytics tools.AI-generated content may be incorrect.][image3]

### **5.4 Project Charter** {#5.4-project-charter}

#### 

| Project Title: 	Webservice for Stock Market Prediction |  |  |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Project Start Date:  1 April 2026                   Projected Finish Date:** 20 August 2026 |  |  |
| **Project Manager:** Chai Ming Liang, \+65 88148980, mlchai001@mymail.sim.edu.sg |  |  |
| **Project Objectives:**  The objective of this project is to develop a web-based stock market forecasting and recommendation service that integrates a frontend application with a machine learning-powered backend engine. The frontend will receive real-time stock market data through RESTful APIs and provide users with accessible forecasting and recommendation services. The backend engine will analyze current and historical stock data using machine learning models to predict stock trends and generate useful investment-related recommendations. To ensure the system remains relevant and accurate, the machine learning models will be designed to update continuously using the latest available stock market data.  |  |  |
| **Success Criteria:** Adherence to scope, schedule, and quality expectations  |  |  |
| **Approach:** The Project will adopt the Scrum framework as the main development approach. The work will be divided into short development cycles called sprints, where the team will plan, design, implement, test and review selected features of the webservice. Sprint planning, weekly meetings, sprint reviews will be conducted to monitor progress and address issues. This approach allows the team to respond quickly to changing requirements.   |  |  |
| **Roles and Responsibilities** |  |  |
| ***Name*** | ***Position / Role*** | ***Contact Information*** |
| Chai Ming Liang | Team Leader, Documentation, Backend Developer | mlchai001@mymail.sim.edu.sg |
| Bali Pratama Tok | Documentation, Frontend Developer | bptok001@mymail.sim.edu.sg |
| Anbu Maithrishree | Documentation, Frontend Developer | anbu002@mymail.sim.edu.sg |
| Chuan Yuhong Addison | Documentation, Backend Developer | yachuan001@mymail.sim.edu.sg |
| Chong Yik Siong Ian | Documentation, Backend Developer | ysichong001@mymail.sim.edu.sg |

### **5.5 Communication Management Plan** {#5.5-communication-management-plan}

#### *5.5.1 Stakeholder Communication Requirements*

| Stakeholders | Communication Channels |
| :---- | :---- |
| Project Team | Discord, Zoom, Telegram, Taiga, Google Drive, GitHub |
| Supervisor | Email, Zoom |
| Assessor | Physical Meetings |

#### *5.5.2 Communications Summary*

| Stakeholders | Communications Name | Delivery Method/Format | Producer | Due/Frequency |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Project Team | Sprint Review and Planning | Discord, Telegram | Team Leader | Weekly |
| Project Team | Task Assignment and updates | Telegram, Taiga | Team Leader | Weekly |
| Supervisor | Progress Updates, Reviews and Feedback | Email, Zoom | Full Team | Biweekly or as needed |
| Assessor | Mid-Project Prototype Demo | Physical Meeting | Full Team |  |
| Assessor | Final Presentation | Physical Meeting | Full Team |  |

### **5.5.3 Communication Guidelines** {#5.5.3-communication-guidelines}

* Discord – Will be the main platform for team collaboration and technical discussions  
* Telegram – Will be used for daily communication, reminders, and urgent updates such as meeting schedules, task deadlines, task assignmennts.  
* Taiga – Will be used to manage the product backlog, sprint planning, user stories, and task tracking throughout the Scrum development process.  
* Email – Will be reserved for formal communication with the supervisor, especially for sending official project updates, important queries, meeting confirmations  
* Zoom – Will be used mainly for online meetings with supervisor, project reviews and project updates.

#### *5.5.4 Conflict Escalation Procedure*

* If any team member faces a project-related issue, disagreement, or task blockage, they should first inform the Team Leader through Telegram or raise the matter during a scheduled Discord discussion.  
* The Team Leader will review the issue and attempt to resolve it within one working day by discussing with the involved members, clarifying responsibilities, and identifying possible solutions.  
* If the issue cannot be resolved internally or may affect important project components such as the frontend application, backend engine, machine learning model, or sprint progress, it will be escalated to the Supervisor via Email with a clear explanation of the problem and any supporting information.  
* For conflicts that may delay project milestones, the team will conduct an internal impact analysis and prepare an action plan, which may include redistributing tasks, adjusting sprint priorities, adding buffer time, or simplifying selected deliverables to maintain project progress.

#### *5.5.5 Document Revision Protocol*

* This Communication plan will be reviewed at the end of each Sprint or as needed.  
* Updates will be made when Communication tools have been replaced or added.  
* All changes to the document will be shared with team members.

### **5.6 Scope Statement** {#5.6-scope-statement}

| Project Title: Webservice for Stock Market Prediction |
| :---- |
| **Product Characteristics and Requirements:** Data Gathering and Processing: Collect comprehensive real estate data such as property characteristics, transaction history and location-based features. Clean and pre-process data for consistency, quality, and usability. Model Development: Select and implement suitable machine learning algorithms. Train and fine-tune predictive models using historical transaction data. Evaluate model performance using metrics User Interface Design: Develop an intuitive and responsive web interface. Enable users to input property details and view instant predictions. Data Visualization: Provide interactive charts and maps showing predicted prices, price trends, and area comparison. Help users make informed decisions through clear visual feedback. Deployment and Accessibility: Host the trained model and frontend on a web platform. Ensure accessibility across major devices and browsers.  |
| **Product User Acceptance Criteria:** Webservice must provide reasonably accurate price predictions Interface must be user-friendly Deliverables must be completed within defined schedule System passes Assessor and supervisor requirements and reviews. **Summary of Project Deliverables** Project management-related deliverables: Project charter, scope statement, WBS, Gantt Chart, final presentation, final technical documentation, final user manual, reflective diary, and any other documents required to manage the project. Product-related deliverables: wireframe, use cases, source code and website  |

# **6\. Requirement Definition** {#6.-requirement-definition}

### **6.1 Stakeholder Identification** {#6.1-stakeholder-identification}

Primary Stakeholders:

* Retail Investors: Require clear and accessible stock price predictions to support informed investment decisions.

* Traders: Need timely market signals, trend analysis to assist short-term trading decisions.

* Financial Analysts: Use prediction outputs, sentiment analysis, and technical indicators to support deeper market evaluation.

* Portfolio Managers: Require forecasting insights to assess potential risks, opportunities, and portfolio allocation decisions.

* General Platform Users: Need an easy-to-use interface that presents stock predictions, market trends, and explanations in a simple format.

Secondary Stakeholders:

* System Admins: Maintain platform functionality, manage user access, monitor system performance, and ensure data security.

* Developers: Build, test, and improve the prediction models, APIs, database, and user interface.

* External Data Providers: Supply stock market data, company financial data, news, and sentiment-related information through APIs.

* Cloud / Hosting Providers: Support the deployment, storage, and availability of the webservice for users.

User Types and Functional Scope:

| User Type | Access Scope |
| :---: | :---: |
| Guest User | View Predictions (Limited), View Dashboard |
| Base User | View Predictions, Search Stocks, Watchlist (Limited) |
| Premium User | View Predictions, Search Stocks, Watchlist, Sentiment Analysis, Customizable Prediction, Download Reports |
| User Admin | Manage User Accounts, Monitor Subscriptions |
| Backend Admin | Manage Stocks, Manage Models |

The platform for predicting stock market trends involves multiple stakeholders, including retail investors who need clear and reliable forecasts to support investment decisions, traders who require timely market signals for short-term opportunities, and financial analysts or portfolio managers who need deeper insights from technical indicators, sentiment analysis, and prediction confidence scores. Each stakeholder has different needs, as some users may only require simple stock predictions, while others need more advanced analysis to support higher-risk or higher-value decisions.

Secondary stakeholders are also important for the success of the platform. System admins maintain platform operations, manage user accounts, and ensure data security. Backend admins and developers are responsible for managing stock data, maintaining prediction models, and improving system accuracy. External data providers supply essential market data, company information, financial news, and sentiment-related data that support the prediction algorithms. Regulatory bodies may also influence the platform by setting expectations around data privacy, responsible financial information, and clear communication that predictions should support decision-making rather than guarantee investment outcomes.

The user classification system divides users into different access levels. Guest users can only view limited stock predictions and the main dashboard. Base users can access basic prediction features, search for stocks, and use a limited watchlist. Premium users receive more advanced features, such as sentiment analysis, customizable predictions, detailed reports, and downloadable prediction outputs. User admins manage accounts and subscriptions, while backend admins manage stock datasets, prediction models, and system performance.

### **6.2 Requirement Gathering Methods** {#6.2-requirement-gathering-methods}

The process of gathering requirements for the stock market prediction webservice used a combination of market research, user story development, technical data analysis, and regulatory consideration. Since the system aims to provide stock predictions, sentiment analysis, and market insights, it was important to understand both user expectations and the technical requirements needed to support accurate forecasting.

Primary research involved analysing existing financial platforms such as Yahoo Finance, TradingView, MarketWatch, and stock brokerage applications to identify common features and possible gaps. This helped determine important functions such as stock search, price charts, prediction display, watchlists, confidence scores, sentiment indicators, and downloadable reports. The competitive analysis also helped identify how the proposed system could differentiate itself by combining technical indicators, sentiment analysis, and prediction results in a simpler and more user-friendly dashboard.

User story development was also used to capture realistic usage scenarios from different user groups. For example, a guest user may only want to view limited stock predictions, while a base user may want to search stocks and save them to a watchlist. A premium user may require deeper insights such as sentiment analysis, customizable predictions, and downloadable reports. These user stories helped define the functional scope of the system and ensured that each user type had access to features that matched their needs.

Technical research was conducted to understand the type of data required for stock prediction. This included historical stock price data, trading volume, technical indicators, company financial information, market news, and sentiment-related data. Public datasets and financial APIs were considered as possible data sources to support the prediction model. This helped shape the system’s technical requirements, including database design, API integration, machine learning model inputs, and prediction output format.

Regulatory and ethical research was also important because the platform deals with financial information and user data. The system should clearly present predictions as decision-support information rather than guaranteed investment advice. Data privacy requirements also need to be considered, especially for user accounts, subscriptions, saved watchlists, and downloaded reports.

* Market Analysis: Platforms such as Yahoo Finance, TradingView, MarketWatch, and brokerage apps reviewed.

* User Stories: Captured realistic usage scenarios for guest users, base users, premium users, user admins, and backend admins.

* Regulatory and Ethical Research: Considered data privacy, responsible use of financial predictions, and clear investment-risk disclaimers.

### **6.3 Requirement Analysis Approach** {#6.3-requirement-analysis-approach}

The requirement analysis approach for the stock market prediction webservice focused on prioritising the most important system features based on user needs, technical feasibility, and business value. Since the platform aims to support users in understanding stock market movements, the most critical requirements are those that allow users to access predictions, search for stocks, view market data, and securely manage their accounts.

Prioritization Framework:

| Priority | Features |
| :---: | :---: |
| Critical | Stock price prediction, stock search, user registration, secure login, prediction dashboard, historical price charts, watchlist, prediction confidence score |
| Base User | Sentiment analysis, customizable prediction settings, downloadable reports, premium subscription features, model performance display, advanced technical indicators |

Evaluation Dimensions:

* User Journey Optimization: The system should be easy for users to navigate, from searching for a stock to viewing predictions, charts, confidence scores, and saved watchlist items.

* Technical Feasibility: Features were assessed based on whether they could be realistically implemented using available stock market APIs, machine learning models, database storage, and web development tools.

* Prediction Value and Accuracy: Priority was given to features that improve the usefulness of the platform, such as reliable prediction outputs, historical data analysis, sentiment indicators, and confidence scores.

* Business Value Impact: Premium features such as sentiment analysis, downloadable reports, and customizable predictions were considered desirable because they support subscription-based value and platform differentiation.

* Security and Data Privacy: Secure login, user account management, were treated as important requirements to ensure user trust and safe platform usage.

### **6.4 Summary of User and System Needs** {#6.4-summary-of-user-and-system-needs}

Analysis of user needs revealed several essential requirements for the success of the stock market prediction webservice. Accurate and understandable stock predictions are important because users rely on the platform to support investment and trading decisions. These predictions should be generated using historical stock data, market trends, technical indicators, and sentiment-related information to provide useful insights rather than simple price displays. To improve user confidence, the system should also present prediction results together with confidence scores, charts, and clear explanations.

A user-friendly experience is also necessary because the platform may be used by users with different levels of financial knowledge. Guest users should be able to view limited predictions and explore the dashboard easily, while base users should be able to search for stocks, save watchlist items, and view basic prediction results. Premium users require more advanced tools such as sentiment analysis, customizable predictions, and downloadable reports. The interface should therefore be simple, responsive, and accessible across desktop and mobile devices.

From a system perspective, the platform requires a reliable technical infrastructure that can support regularly updated stock data, user account management, prediction model processing, and secure data storage. The system must be able to integrate external stock market APIs, process historical data, and produce prediction outputs efficiently. Security is also important, especially for user login, subscription management, saved watchlists. Since the platform provides financial prediction support, it should also clearly communicate that predictions are informational and do not guarantee investment outcomes.

User Needs:

* Accurate Predictions: Based on historical stock prices, market trends, technical indicators, and sentiment data.

* Interactive Dashboard: Clear display of stock charts, prediction results, confidence scores, and market movement indicators.

* Stock Search and Watchlist: Users can search for stocks and save selected stocks for easier monitoring.

* Account Management: Easy registration, secure login, profile management, and subscription upgrade or downgrade options.

* Advanced Analysis Tools: Premium users can access sentiment analysis, customizable predictions, and downloadable reports.

* Responsive Design: Platform should work smoothly across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.

System Requirements:

* Role-Based Access Control: Different access levels for guest users, base users, premium users, user admins, and backend admins.

* Scalable Infrastructure: Able to handle multiple users, stock searches, prediction requests, and dashboard activity.

* Secure Data Pipeline: Integration with external stock APIs, financial news sources, and sentiment data while protecting user information.

* Machine Learning Prediction Model: Generates stock trend or price predictions using historical and market-related data.

* Model Management: Backend admins can manage stock datasets, update prediction models, and monitor model performance.

* Data Security and Privacy: Secure login, protected user accounts, safe storage of watchlists, and responsible handling of subscription data.

### **6.5 Use of Tools** {#6.5-use-of-tools}

Use Case Diagrams were used to support the requirement analysis and system design of the stock market prediction webservice. These tools helped clarify how different user roles interact with the system and how the main technical components connect with one another.

Use Case Diagrams show the interaction between users and platform features based on their access levels. For example, guest users can only view limited predictions and the dashboard, while base users can search stocks and manage a limited watchlist. Premium users have access to advanced features such as sentiment analysis, customizable predictions, and downloadable reports. Admin users are responsible for managing user accounts, subscriptions, stock data, and prediction models. This helps ensure that stakeholder needs and role-based access control are clearly defined.

* Use Case Diagrams: Show user-role-based access to prediction features, stock search, watchlist, sentiment analysis, downloadable reports, and admin functions.

* System Architecture Diagrams: Show the connection between frontend, backend, database, external stock APIs, sentiment analysis, and machine learning models.

* Data Flow Diagrams: Show how stock data, sentiment data, and user requests move through the system to produce prediction results.

# **7\. Functional Requirements** {#7.-functional-requirements}

### **7.1 Functional Hierarchy** {#7.1-functional-hierarchy}

The StockLens webservice is organized into several functional modules to support stock analysis, prediction generation, user interaction, visualization, and system administration. The functional hierarchy ensures modular system development and easier maintenance of the platform.

The major functional modules of the system are:

1. User Management Module   
2. Stock Search and Data Input   
3. Prediction and Financial Tools   
4. Visualization and Reports   
5. System Administration Console

**7.1.1 User Management Module**

The User Management Module manages authentication, account security, profile management, and subscription handling for all users of the system.

**Functions:**

* Account registration   
* User login and logout   
* Password update and reset   
* Profile viewing and updating   
* Subscription management   
* Premium membership upgrade   
* Session management   
* Role-based access control 

This module provides secure authentication and personalized user access to the StockLens platform. Users can create accounts, manage their profiles, and access features based on their subscription tier.

**7.1.2 Stock Search and Data Input**

The Stock Search and Data Input module allows users to retrieve stock-related information and input data required for prediction analysis.

**Functions:**

* Stock ticker search   
* Company name search   
* Historical stock data retrieval   
* Favourite stock management   
* Watchlist management   
* Real-time market data retrieval   
* Stock category filtering   
* User-customized prediction input 

They retrieves financial data from APIs and databases to support stock analysis and prediction generation. Users can search for stocks, manage watchlists, and retrieve historical market information for analysis.

**7.1.3 Prediction and Financial Tools**

The Prediction and Financial Tools module is the core analytical component of the system that generates stock market predictions using machine learning and financial analysis techniques.

**Functions:**

* Stock price prediction   
* Buy / Sell / Hold signal generation   
* Technical analysis processing   
* Sentiment analysis   
* Confidence score generation   
* Prediction comparison   
* Customizable prediction settings   
* Combined prediction model generation 

This processes stock market data, technical indicators, and sentiment analysis data to generate predictive insights. Machine learning models such as LSTM and XGBoost are used to improve prediction accuracy and produce confidence scores.

**7.1.4 Visualization and Reports**

The Visualization and Reports module presents prediction results and stock analysis information through dashboards, charts, and downloadable reports.

**Functions:**

* Interactive stock dashboard   
* Historical stock trend visualization   
* Prediction accuracy charts   
* Sentiment analysis visualization   
* Watchlist analytics   
* PDF and CSV report generation   
* Performance metrics display 

This module enhances user understanding by displaying stock market analytics and prediction results visually. Users can analyze market trends and prediction performance through charts, dashboards, and reports.

**7.1.5 System Administration Console** 

The System Administration Console provides backend administrative control for managing users, prediction models, and system operations.

Functions:

* User account management   
* Account suspension and reactivation   
* Subscription management   
* Prediction model configuration   
* Market sentiment watchlist management   
* Report generation   
* Audit log monitoring 

This module enables administrators to monitor and manage the StockLens platform. Administrators can control user access, configure prediction algorithms, and generate reports for system maintenance and monitoring purposes.

### **7.2 Basic Features Access Levels** {#7.2-basic-features-access-levels}

## 

| Features | Guest User | Base User | Premium User | User Admin | Backend Admin |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Register Account | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Login / Logout | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Search Stocks | Limited | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| View Predictions | Limited | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Favourite / Watchlist Management | ✗ | Limited | Unlimited | ✓ | ✓ |
| Sentiment Analysis | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Customizable Predictions | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| View Dashboard | Limited | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Download Reports | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Manage User Accounts | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Configure Prediction Models | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
| Generate System Reports | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |

## 

### **7.3 Dependencies** {#7.3-dependencies}

The StockLens system depends on several software frameworks, APIs, libraries, and services to support system functionality.

| Dependency | Purpose |
| ----- | ----- |
| React.js | Frontend user interface development |
| Flask REST API | Backend service and API handling |
| Supabase PostgreSQL | Database management and authentication |
| TensorFlow | Machine learning model training |
| PyTorch | Deep learning implementation |
| LSTM Model | Sequential stock price prediction |
| XGBoost | Structured prediction analysis |
| yfinance API | Stock market data retrieval |
| FinBERT / VADER | Sentiment analysis |
| RESTful APIs | Frontend-backend communication |
| Vercel / Render | Cloud deployment |
| Visual Studio Code | Development environment |
| GanttPro | Project scheduling and management |

The system depends on machine learning frameworks, financial APIs, and cloud hosting services to retrieve market data, process prediction algorithms, and provide real-time stock analysis functionality. 

### **7.4 Inputs and Outputs**  {#7.4-inputs-and-outputs}

| Module | Inputs | Outputs |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| User Management | Registration details, login credentials, profile data | User accounts, authentication status |
| Stock Search | Stock ticker, company name, search filters | Matching stock information |
| Prediction Engine | Historical stock prices, technical indicators, sentiment data | Predicted stock price, confidence score |
| Sentiment Analysis | News articles, tweets, social media content | Positive, neutral, negative sentiment |
| Visualization Dashboard | Prediction results, analytical data | Charts, dashboards, reports |
| Watchlist Management | Favourite stock selections | Personalized watchlist |
| Administration Console | User management actions, system settings | Updated system configurations |
| Reporting System | Report type, selected date range | PDF or CSV reports |

The system processes financial market data, user inputs, and sentiment information to generate stock predictions and analytical reports. Outputs are displayed through dashboards, charts, and downloadable report files to support investment decision-making.

# **8\. Non-functional Requirements** {#8.-non-functional-requirements}

### **8.1 Performance Requirements** {#8.1-performance-requirements}

Respone Time Standards

| Operation Type | Target Response Time | Success Rate |
| :---: | :---: | :---: |
| Stock Predictions | \< 10 Seconds | 70% |
| Page Load Times | \< 2 Seconds | 100% |
| Custom Predictions | \< 20 Seconds | 65% |
| Database Queries | \< 2 Seconds | 99% |
| User Authentication | \< 1 Second | 99% |

Performance requirements establish the technical standards needed to ensure a smooth and reliable user experience across the stock market prediction platform. Stock prediction results should be generated within three seconds for most user requests, especially when users search for a stock and request forecast outputs. Static pages such as the homepage, login page, and dashboard should load within two seconds, while interactive stock charts and historical price visualisations should load within five seconds.

User interaction database queries, such as login, registration, watchlist updates, and stock searches, should respond quickly to avoid delays in the user journey. The system should also support multiple concurrent users, especially during market hours when users are more likely to search for stocks, view predictions, and monitor watchlists.

Premium features such as sentiment analysis, customizable predictions, and downloadable reports should support multiple simultaneous requests without affecting the performance of basic platform functions. The infrastructure should be scalable to support future growth in user numbers, stock coverage, and prediction requests.

The database must be able to manage large volumes of historical stock prices, trading volume, user watchlists, prediction results, and sentiment data. The machine learning component should support regular model updates and produce predictions within acceptable response times. Overall, the system should balance prediction accuracy, fast response time, and platform reliability to provide users with effective decision-support information.

### **8.2 Security Requirements** {#8.2-security-requirements}

Highlights:

* Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)

* Database Encryption for sensitive user and subscription information

* Secure API Key Management for external stock data providers

* PDPA Compliance: Consent, transparency, and responsible data handling

* Password Hashing and Secure Authentication

Data Protection Standards:

The stock market prediction webservice must protect sensitive user information through secure data storage, encrypted database records, and strong authentication practices. Since the platform may store user accounts, watchlists, subscription details, and downloaded report history, these data must be protected from unauthorised access. Passwords should not be stored in plain text and should instead be hashed using secure password-hashing methods.

Role-based access control is required to ensure that each user type can only access features assigned to their role. Guest users should only access limited prediction features, base users should access standard prediction and watchlist functions, while premium users can access advanced features such as sentiment analysis, customizable predictions, and downloadable reports. User admins should manage user accounts and subscriptions, while backend admins should manage stock datasets and machine learning models. This prevents ordinary users from accessing administrative or backend functions.

The system should also secure API integrations because the platform depends on external stock market data, financial news, and sentiment-related sources. API keys should be stored securely in environment variables and should not be exposed in frontend code. Communication between the frontend, backend, database, and external APIs should be protected using HTTPS to reduce the risk of data interception.

In accordance with data privacy principles such as Singapore’s Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA), the platform should manage user consent for account creation, subscription services, and data collection. Users should be informed about what personal data is collected, how it is used, and how it is protected. Since the system provides stock market predictions, it should also clearly state that predictions are for decision-support purposes only and do not guarantee investment outcomes.

### **8.3 Interface and Usability Requirements** {#8.3-interface-and-usability-requirements}

UI Design:

* Responsive UI: Supports desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.

* Cross-Browser Compatibility: Supports major browsers such as Chrome and Safari.

* Simple Financial Dashboard: Clear layout for stock predictions, charts, sentiment results, and confidence scores.

Usability Metrics:

* Task completion rate: \>90%

* Onboarding success: \>80%

* User satisfaction: \>4.2 / 5

* Support contact rate: \<5%

Interface and usability requirements establish standards for how users interact with the stock market prediction webservice. Since the platform is designed to support investment-related decision-making, the interface must be clear, professional, and easy to understand. Users should be able to search for stocks, view prediction results, analyse charts, manage watchlists, and access reports without needing advanced technical knowledge.

The user interface should use a responsive design that works smoothly across desktop computers, tablets, and mobile devices. This ensures that users can check market predictions and stock information regardless of their preferred device. The system should also support major browsers such as Chrome and Safari to improve accessibility for a wider range of users.

Navigation should be simple and efficient. Core functions such as stock search, prediction dashboard, watchlist, sentiment analysis, login, and subscription management should be reachable within three clicks. This reduces user frustration and improves the overall user journey. For financial platforms, users need information quickly, so the dashboard should present prediction outputs, historical stock charts, confidence scores, and market signals in a structured and readable format.

Cross-browser compatibility:

* Supports major browsers such as Chrome and Safari for wider user access.

* Maintains consistent layout, chart display, and dashboard functionality across different screen sizes and browsers.

* Allows users to reach key features such as stock search, prediction results, watchlist, and reports within three clicks.

Usability metrics:

* Onboarding completion rates should exceed 80%, ensuring most new users can register, log in, and understand the platform successfully.

* Task completion rates for core functions such as searching stocks, viewing predictions, saving watchlist items, and downloading reports should exceed 90%.

User satisfaction scores and ratings:

* User satisfaction scores should average above 4.0 out of 5.0, showing that users find the platform useful, understandable, and reliable.

* Support contact rates should remain below 5% of active users, indicating that the interface is intuitive and does not require frequent assistance.

Design specifications:

* The platform should use a minimalistic and professional design suitable for financial decision-making.

* Consistent colours, fonts, icons, and chart styles should be used to reduce confusion and improve readability.

* Prediction results should be clearly labelled, including stock symbol, predicted trend, forecast price, confidence score.

* Loading indicators should appear for tasks taking longer than one second, such as generating predictions, loading charts, running sentiment analysis, or downloading reports.

* Error messages should be clear and actionable. For example, if stock data cannot be loaded, the system should explain the issue and suggest retrying instead of showing a generic error.

### **8,4 Portability and Scalability Requirements** {#8,4-portability-and-scalability-requirements}

Portability and scalability requirements ensure that the stock market prediction webservice can adapt to technical changes, increasing user demand, and future system expansion while maintaining stable performance.

Portability:

The platform uses GitHub for code versioning, collaboration, and progress tracking. This allows developers to manage changes, review code, and maintain a clear development history. The system should be designed so that it can run across different development and deployment environments with minimal changes. Environment variables should be used to store sensitive configuration details such as database URLs, API keys, and authentication secrets.

Scalability:

The platform is designed to support future growth in the number of users, stock symbols, prediction requests, and data sources. As the system expands, it should be able to handle more users searching stocks, viewing dashboards, managing watchlists, and generating prediction reports at the same time.

The system should also be scalable in terms of data coverage. In the early stage, the platform may focus on selected stocks or markets, but it should be designed to support future expansion into more stock exchanges, more financial indicators, company fundamentals, market news, and real-time data streams. Future features such as advanced charts, portfolio tracking, alert notifications, and feedback tools can also be added as separate modules.

# **9\. Other Requirements** {#9.-other-requirements}

Beyond the functional and non-functional requirements, there are additional considerations that are important for the success of the stock market prediction webservice. These requirements focus on how the system is developed, maintained, operated, and used responsibly. Since the platform deals with financial information, prediction outputs, user accounts, and external market data, the system must be designed with strong technical reliability, clear user communication, and responsible data handling.

These requirements also help ensure that the platform can continue to improve over time. The system should be flexible enough to support future model updates, additional stock markets, new data sources, and extra features such as alerts, portfolio tracking, or advanced reporting. At the same time, the platform must remain easy to maintain, secure to operate, and transparent to users.

The following subsections provide detailed explanations of the technical, user, legal and regulatory, system, and operational considerations for the stock market prediction webservice. These considerations support the long-term development of the platform and ensure that it can provide useful stock prediction insights while managing risk, privacy, and system performance effectively.

### **9.1 User Requirements** {#9.1-user-requirements}

Our system has several types of users, including Guest Users, Base Users, Premium Users, User Admins, and Backend Admins. Each user group has different access levels based on their role and subscription status.

* Guest users must be able to browse basic platform features without creating an account. They should be able to view the homepage, read project information, view sample stock predictions, access FAQs, and compare available subscription plans.

* Base users should be able to register an account, log in securely, search for stocks, view basic stock predictions, access historical price charts, and save a limited number of stocks to their watchlist. They should also be able to manage their profile information and view basic prediction history.

* Premium users must have access to advanced prediction features such as sentiment analysis, confidence scores, customizable prediction settings, detailed stock analysis, and downloadable prediction reports. They should also be able to manage their subscription, upgrade or downgrade plans, and access a larger or unlimited watchlist.

* User admins must be able to manage user-related activities, including viewing user accounts, updating user information, managing subscription status, handling account issues, and suspending or deactivating accounts when necessary.

* Backend admins must be able to manage the technical side of the platform, including stock datasets, external API connections, prediction model updates, sentiment analysis data, and system performance monitoring. They should also be able to review system logs and ensure that prediction services are working correctly.

Overall, the system must provide a simple and user-friendly interface that allows users to navigate easily between stock search, prediction results, watchlists, reports, and account settings. The platform should support both desktop and mobile devices, provide clear instructions for new users, and include or support options to help users understand how to use the prediction features responsibly.

### **9.2 Legal and Regulatory Requirements** {#9.2-legal-and-regulatory-requirements}

The system must comply with relevant data protection, financial information, and responsible technology requirements. Since the stock market prediction webservice collects user account information and provides prediction-based financial insights, the platform must ensure that user data is protected and that prediction results are presented responsibly.

**Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) Compliance:**

The platform must collect only essential user information, such as name, email address, login details, subscription status, and saved watchlist data. User consent should be obtained before collecting, storing, or using personal data.

**Data Protection and Encryption:**

Sensitive user information should be protected through secure storage, encryption, and password hashing. User passwords must not be stored in plain text, and access to personal data should be restricted to authorised users only.

**Financial Disclaimer:**

The platform must clearly state that stock predictions are estimates generated using historical data, technical indicators, sentiment analysis, and machine learning models. The predictions should not be treated as guaranteed results or professional financial advice.

**Terms of Use and Privacy Policy:**

The platform should include clear Terms of Use and Privacy Policy pages. These documents should explain how the system works, what data is collected, how user data is protected, and the limitations of using prediction-based outputs for investment decisions.

**Responsible Use of External Data:**

The system must ensure that external stock market data, financial news, and sentiment data are used according to the terms and conditions of the selected data providers. API keys and third-party data access must be handled securely.

**User Consent and Transparency:**

Users should be informed about how their data is used, including account management, watchlist storage, subscription features, and prediction history. Users should also be able to update their account information or deactivate their account when necessary.

### **9.3 System and Operation Requirements** {#9.3-system-and-operation-requirements}

The stock market prediction webservice should be designed to operate reliably, scale as usage increases, and support future improvements without requiring major redevelopment. Since the platform handles stock data, prediction requests, user accounts, watchlists, and premium reports, it must provide a smooth and stable user experience while maintaining security and system performance.

* The system must support high concurrency with minimal downtime.

  * The platform should aim for at least 99% uptime, especially during active market periods when users are more likely to search for stocks, view predictions, and monitor watchlists.

* The prediction engine must return results within 3 seconds for standard stock prediction requests.

  * When users search for a stock and request a prediction, the system should process the input, retrieve relevant data, run the prediction model, and display the result within an acceptable response time.

* The platform must be mobile-responsive and cross-browser compatible.

  * Users should be able to access the platform smoothly on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices. The system should also support major browsers such as Chrome and Safari.

* The backend must be scalable to handle more users and requests.

  * As the number of users increases, the backend should be able to manage more stock searches, prediction requests, user logins, watchlist updates, and report downloads without major performance issues.

* The system should be modular for easy future expansion.

  * Core components such as user management, stock data collection, sentiment analysis, prediction model processing, subscription management, and report generation should be separated. This allows new features to be added without changing the entire system.

* The platform should support future expansion into more stock markets and data sources.

  * The system may initially focus on selected stocks or exchanges, but it should be designed to support additional markets, more stock symbols, company fundamentals, financial news, and real-time data streams in the future.

* The system must provide a secure admin panel for monitoring and maintenance.

  * User admins should be able to manage accounts and subscriptions, while backend admins should be able to monitor prediction model performance, manage datasets, check API connections, and review system logs.

* The system must support regular maintenance and model updates.

  * The machine learning model should be updated or retrained when new data becomes available. This ensures that prediction outputs remain relevant and useful over time.

* The system should handle errors clearly and safely.

  * If stock data cannot be retrieved, the prediction model fails, or an external API is unavailable, the platform should display clear error messages and prevent system crashes.

# **10\. Risk Management**

Managing risks is a fundamental part of developing any software system, especially one that relies heavily on external data sources and machine learning, such as our StockLens web service for stock market prediction. The success of this project relies on several important factors, including the reliability of the yfinance data pipeline, the accuracy of the machine learning models, system security, and the trust of our users.

Since the platform involves integrating real-time stock market data with predictive modelling and user-facing features, we need to consider various risks including technical, legal, operational, and user-related factors. If these risks are not handled properly, they could cause major consequences, such as inaccurate stock price predictions, data security breaches, violating regulations like Singapore's PDPA, or a lack of user engagement.

To prevent these issues, we are taking a proactive approach. This includes identifying potential risks early, assessing how likely and how serious they might be, and putting the right measures in place to manage or reduce their impact. This risk management strategy will help us track project health, address problems before they escalate, and ensure that the platform stays reliable, secure, and legally compliant.

 

| Risk | Description | Impact | Likelihood | Mitigation Plan |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Data Source Reliability Risk | The system depends on the yfinance API for real-time and historical stock data. If the API becomes unavailable, rate-limited, or returns inaccurate data, predictions will be unreliable. | High | Medium | Implement data validation checks before processing, cache historical data locally, and build fallback mechanisms to alert users when live data is temporarily unavailable. |
| Machine Learning Model Performance Risk | The prediction models (XGBoost, LSTM, FinBERT) may fail to deliver accurate results under certain market conditions such as high volatility or black swan events, affecting user trust. | High | Medium | Conduct iterative model training and testing with diverse market datasets, apply cross-validation, display model confidence scores, and collect user feedback for continuous improvement. |
| Legal & Regulatory Compliance Risk | Breach of the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) or failure to include adequate financial disclaimers under the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) Financial Advisers Act could lead to legal consequences. | Critical | Low | Ensure user data collection is consent-based, enforce strict data protection policies through Supabase Row Level Security and encryption, display clear disclaimers that predictions are for educational purposes only, and implement Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. |
| System Security Risk | Unauthorized access or hacking could lead to data leaks or service disruption, affecting both user trust and system integrity. | Critical | Low | Implement secure authentication via Supabase Auth, enforce HTTPS, apply rate limiting on API endpoints, and encrypt sensitive user data at rest and in transit. |
| Integration Risk | Failure in integrating the React frontend, Flask backend, ML prediction engine, and Supabase database may cause functionality breakdown or user experience issues. | Medium | Medium | Apply continuous integration practices, conduct unit and integration testing at every stage, and ensure backend APIs are stable and documented before frontend connection. |
| Hosting & Server Downtime Risk | Unexpected server failures or poor performance on Vercel (frontend) or the backend hosting platform may disrupt user access, leading to dissatisfaction and loss of users. | High | Low | Host on reliable cloud platforms, implement health monitoring and automated alerts, and conduct regular performance testing to identify bottlenecks early. |
| User Adoption & Usability Risk | Poor UI/UX design may result in low user engagement, particularly if stock data visualisations or prediction outputs are difficult to interpret. | Medium | Medium | Focus on user-centred design principles, conduct usability testing with target users, gather early feedback, and iterate on the interface based on user input. |
| Scope Creep & Timeline Risk | Expanding project scope beyond initial planning could lead to delays and resource strain, particularly given the fixed FYP submission deadlines. | High | Medium | Use Agile project management with fixed sprint timelines via Taiga, monitor progress regularly through stand-ups and sprint reviews, and enforce a strict change request process. |
| Technical Expertise Risk | The team may face technical challenges, especially in ML model deployment, API integration, or real-time data pipeline implementation. | Medium | Medium | Provide team training sessions, encourage collaboration and pair programming, leverage online resources and documentation, and seek supervisor guidance when necessary. |

 

# **11\. Development Methodologies**

Our project uses Scrum as the main development approach, with Taiga as the management tool and Microsoft Excel for Gantt chart tracking. We chose this combination because it gives us a balance of flexibility, structure, and clear communication, which is important for a project that involves both machine learning modelling and frontend interface design.

**Adaptability and Iteration**

Scrum breaks work into short sprints. Each sprint focuses on a small set of tasks, so the team can deliver results step by step instead of waiting until the end. At the end of every sprint, we review our progress in Taiga and decide what to improve for the next cycle. In practice, this means we can quickly refine our prediction models or test new features like stock comparison tools or sentiment analysis without getting stuck on one long development cycle.

**Task Tracking**

One of the reasons we chose Taiga is because it makes task management simple to follow. The Kanban board and backlog show exactly what stage each task is at. For example, data pipeline development might be "in progress," while the authentication module might already be marked as "done." This overview helps the team avoid confusion and keeps deadlines in sight.

**Workflows**

The project is divided into clear phases: data gathering and exploration, model development, frontend and backend integration, and testing. Taiga lets us customise these workflows so they fit the actual order of our work. If new requirements appear, such as adding premium features or additional ML models, we can insert them into the workflow without disrupting the rest of the plan.

**Collaboration**

Since not all members can always meet in person, we rely on Taiga's collaboration tools to stay connected. Features like comments, file uploads, and notifications make it easier to update one another. This is useful for coordinating between the ML team, the backend developers, and the frontend group, especially when working remotely.

**Prioritisation**

Scrum also helps the team focus on tasks that deliver the most value. By using Taiga's backlog, we can rank features based on importance. For example, improving prediction accuracy and building the core stock search functionality are treated as higher priorities compared to smaller interface adjustments. This way, we are always working on items that matter most to users.

**User Stories**

To make sure development stays connected to user needs, we use user stories. Examples include "As a Base User, I want to search for a stock by ticker symbol so that I can view its prediction" and "As a Premium User, I want to customise prediction parameters so that I can explore different scenarios." Each story is linked to specific tasks in Taiga so that all work leads back to a real requirement.

**Feedback and Improvement**

Finally, Scrum encourages continuous feedback. At the end of each sprint, we conduct reviews and retrospectives through Taiga. This allows us to spot problems early, whether they relate to model performance, data pipeline stability, or the usability of the interface. It also creates room for improvement throughout the project instead of leaving changes until the end.

# **12\. Technical Stack**

## **12.1 Frontend Framework**

The system uses React with Vite as the frontend framework to build a dynamic and interactive user interface for the stock market prediction platform.

➔  React's component-based architecture and efficient state management suit handling user input, real-time stock data display, and data visualisations. Vite provides a significantly faster development experience with instant hot module replacement and optimised production builds. The large React ecosystem and compatibility with charting libraries like Recharts and Chart.js simplify integrating interactive stock price charts and prediction graphs.

While alternatives like:

➔  Vue.js offers simplicity and a gentle learning curve, it has a smaller ecosystem and fewer third-party libraries for complex financial data visualisation;

➔  Vanilla JS lacks the scalability and component reusability needed for a multi-page application with diverse user roles; and

➔  Next.js focuses on server-side rendering and static site generation, which adds unnecessary complexity for a single-page application that primarily consumes API data.

Therefore, React with Vite was selected for its flexibility, rich ecosystem, fast build tooling, and ability to support the complex UI interactions required in this project.

## **12.2 Backend Framework**

The system uses Flask as the backend framework due to its simplicity and flexibility in building lightweight RESTful APIs for the stock market prediction platform.

➔  Flask's minimalistic design enables rapid development and easy customisation without unnecessary overhead, making it ideal for serving Python-based machine learning models and efficiently handling user requests. Flask integrates naturally with the ML stack (TensorFlow, XGBoost, HuggingFace Transformers) since they share the Python runtime.

The backend serves three core ML components:

➔  **Sentiment Analysis (FinBERT):** A pre-trained financial domain BERT model from HuggingFace Transformers, used to analyse market sentiment from news headlines and financial text. FinBERT is specifically fine-tuned on financial communication data, making it more accurate than general-purpose sentiment tools for stock market context.

➔  **Technical Analysis (LSTM):** A Long Short-Term Memory neural network implemented using TensorFlow/Keras, used for time-series stock price prediction based on historical price patterns and technical indicators.

➔  **Financial Forecast (XGBoost):** An XGBoost gradient boosting model using the xgboost library with scikit-learn API, used for stock price forecasting based on engineered features including volume, moving averages, and momentum indicators.

While alternatives like:

➔  Django offers a full-featured framework but adds unnecessary complexity and overhead for a project that primarily serves as an API layer;

➔  FastAPI provides high performance and modern async features but has a steeper learning curve and may be more than required for the current project scope; and

➔  Node.js \+ Express is flexible and performant but requires switching to JavaScript for backend development, which adds significant overhead since all ML components are Python-based.

Therefore, Flask was selected for its lightweight nature, seamless integration with Python machine learning libraries, and straightforward API development aligned with the project's scope.

## **12.3 Database**

The system uses Supabase, an open-source Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS) platform built on top of PostgreSQL, as the primary database and authentication solution. Supabase provides a managed PostgreSQL database with built-in authentication, real-time subscriptions, and auto-generated RESTful APIs, making it well-suited for rapid development of the StockLens platform.

➔  **Relational Structure:** Stock market data (tickers, historical prices, predictions, user watchlists) is best modelled in structured tables with relations (e.g., user → watchlist → stock).

➔  **Built-in Authentication:** Supabase Auth provides secure user registration, login, email verification, and session management out of the box, eliminating the need to build a custom authentication system.

➔  **Row Level Security (RLS):** PostgreSQL's RLS policies enforced through Supabase ensure that users can only access their own data (e.g., watchlists, favourites), providing fine-grained access control at the database level.

➔  **Real-time Capabilities:** Supabase's real-time subscriptions allow the frontend to receive instant updates when data changes, useful for live stock data feeds and notification systems.

➔  **Auto-generated APIs:** Supabase automatically generates RESTful and GraphQL APIs from the database schema, speeding up development and reducing boilerplate backend code.

A variety of database alternatives were assessed for the stock market prediction system, ultimately selecting Supabase (PostgreSQL). Nevertheless, options like Firebase, MongoDB Atlas, and PlanetScale were also taken into account.

➔  **Firebase** is noted for its real-time synchronisation and developer-friendly frontend integration, yet it uses a NoSQL document model that is less suited for the relational queries required for stock data analysis, user-watchlist relationships, and structured prediction logs.

➔  **MongoDB Atlas** excels in handling unstructured data and horizontal scaling, but does not natively support relational integrity or complex JOIN operations needed for linking users, stocks, predictions, and watchlists efficiently.

➔  **PlanetScale** offers a serverless MySQL-compatible database with excellent scalability, but lacks the built-in authentication, Row Level Security, and auto-generated API features that Supabase provides, requiring additional development effort for these capabilities.

These comparisons led to the choice of Supabase due to its managed PostgreSQL foundation, built-in authentication and Row Level Security, auto-generated APIs, generous free tier suitable for an FYP project, and seamless integration with the React frontend through the Supabase JavaScript client library.

# **10\. References** {#10.-references}
